An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  heat to constant mass/ until mass does not change  to ensure all the water is lost/ driven off (1) (1) Allow heat for longer Ignore any reference to repetition/ using a higher temperature/different flame/more magnesium sulfate/ any ch anges to the method Do not award heat unde r reflux for longer Allow more water is lost/given off Allow some water may have remained Allow all the water evaporated Ignore allow (reaction) to go to completion (2) (Total for Question 19 = 7 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points  Cu ([Ar]) 3d 4s  Cu 2+ ([Ar]) 3d (1) (1) ALLOW 4s 3d ALLOW 4s 3d Ignore 1s 2s 2p 3s 3p in both cases (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark Isotope Protons Neutrons Electrons Cu 29 34 Cu 29 36 (1) (1) One mark for each correct row Four or five correct scores one mark I g nore workin g (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points:  (atoms/elements that have) same number of protons/same proton number/ quoted same number of protons even if wrong  (but) different numbers of neutrons/neutron number (1) (1) Ignore any reference to elec trons but do not award if different to the number of protons Ignore if they state the wrong number of neutrons in the 2 isotopes. If they fail to mention numbers of protons and neutrons ‘same atomic number but diff erent mass number’ scores (1) Do not award atomic mass for mass number Do not award molecules but penalise once only (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark  (isotopes have) the same electronic configuration Allow same electron arrangement/electron(ic) structure Allow the same number of electrons Ignore the same number of protons Ignore the same number of electrons in the outer shell/same number of valence electrons Ignore same peri od/same group Ignore any given electronic configurations/number of electrons even if wrong Ignore any reference to neutrons (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  expression relating isotopic masses A r and X  abundance of (Cu) 63 = 0.8 or 80% abundance of (Cu) 65 = 0.2 or 20% Alternative method 65- 63.4 = 1.6 63.4-63 = 0.4 0.4/2.0 × 100 = 20% 65 1.6/2.0 × 100 = 80% 63 (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 63.4 = (1 − X) × 65) + (X × 63) 1 63.4 = 65 – 65X + 63X 63.4 = 65 – 2X − 1.6 = − 2X X / 63 = 0.8 OR 63.4 = (1 − X) × 63) + (X × 65) 1 63.4 = 63 – 63X + 65X 63.4 = 63 + 2X 0.4 = 2X X / 65 = 0.2 (2) OR 63.4 = (100 − X) × 65) + (X × 63) 100 6

An answer that makes reference to two of the following points: o M1 (General increase because there is the same positive charge) but fewer electrons/ less repulsion between elections/ electrons getting closer to the nucleus/ ion becoming increasingly more positive/increase in effective nuclear charge o M2 there is a big jump between 5 th -6 th ionisation energies as the 6 th electron is removed from a new shell/ quantum shell/ energy level (closer to the nucleus) o M3 there is a jump between 3 rd - 4 th ionisation energies as the 4 th electron is removed from a new sub shell/2s (closer to the nucleus) (1) (1) (1) Allow there is a big jump between 5 th -6 th ionisation energies as nitrogen has 5 electrons in its outer shell Ignore it is in group 5 Allow there is a big jump between 5 th -6 th ionisation energies as the inner electrons have no shielding (2) (Total for Question 23 = 7 marks) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark
